summary: eth-lib-utils impersonates ethereumjs-util and silently pulls suspicious transitive dependency
details: Package name eth-lib-utils impersonates the established @ethereumjs/util / ethereumjs-util package — README, homepage, repository, and contributor list all reference the ethereumjs/ethereumjs-monorepo project, but the package is published by an unrelated author. The main entry dist/index.js contains a side-effect-only require("assertcoreutils") at module load time, and package.json declares "assertcoreutils": "^2.3.2" as a dependency. The genuine ethereumjs-util does not depend on any package named assertcoreutils (it uses ethjs-util); the name assertcoreutils is itself shaped like a typosquat against core-util-is / assert. Any developer who installs eth-lib-utils believing it is ethereumjs-util will, on first require('eth-lib-utils'), execute the top-level code of the attacker-introduced assertcoreutils transitive in the installer's process, giving the publisher a code-execution channel into the installer's dependency graph.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
